content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — NSCameraUsageDescription is generic and NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription is a literal "TODO" placeholder (Info.plist:6-9), while no location usage exists anywhere in code (ScannerView.swift only uses AVCaptureDevice for camera).

Pierre: Apple requires purpose strings to explain specifically why the app needs the permission and how it's used, and reviewers will reject "TODO" or boilerplate text on sight. Here the camera string doesn't mention document scanning (the actual feature in ScannerView.swift) and the location string is an unfinished placeholder for a permission that's never actually used in the codebase — remove the unused location key entirely, or if location is needed elsewhere, write a real description and wire it to a visible feature.
